scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Dairy Plus Programme (Provision of 02 Murrah Buffaloes on Subsidy)
page_title: Mukhyamantri Dairy Plus Programme (Provision of 02 Murrah Buffaloes on Subsidy)

details: The "Mukhyamantri Dairy Plus Programme (Provision of 02 Murrah Buffaloes on Subsidy)" scheme is implemented by the Animal Husbandry Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme aims to enhance milk production, promote improved livestock breeding, and support small and marginal livestock farmers by providing two high genetic quality, pregnant Murrah buffaloes (five months or more), each yielding an average of 8 to 10 liters of milk per day, on a subsidy basis to ensure early productivity.
benefits: General Category: 50% subsidy and 50% beneficiary contribution.
Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), and Scheduled Caste farmers: 75% subsidy and 25% beneficiary contribution.
Provision of 2 Murrah Buffaloes with high genetic quality, each yielding 8–10 liters of milk daily.
Pregnant buffaloes (5 months or more) are provided to ensure immediate milk production.
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
The applicant must be a resident of Sehore, Vidisha, or Raisen district, where the scheme is currently implemented.
The applicant must be a livestock farmer.
The applicant must belong to the General, Schedule Caste, or Schedule Tribe category.
The applicant must have basic infrastructure for maintaining two dairy animals, such as a shed and water availability.

offline: The interested applicant should visit the Veterinary Officer, Veterinary Hospital In-Charge, or Deputy Director of Veterinary Services in their respective district (Sehore, Vidisha, or Raisen) and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the Veterinary Officer, Veterinary Hospital In-Charge, or Deputy Director of Veterinary Services office.

Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the Veterinary Officer, Veterinary Hospital In-Charge, or Deputy Director of Veterinary Services office to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
The application is first approved by the Gram Sabha of the applicant’s village.
The list of Gram Sabha-approved beneficiaries is then forwarded to the Janpad Panchayat meeting for further approval.
After approval at the Janpad Panchayat level, the application is finalized by the District Panchayat Agricultural Standing Committee.
Once approved, two Murrah buffaloes are provided to the selected beneficiary at the prescribed subsidy rate.

faqs.question: In which districts is the scheme active?
faqs.answer: It is implemented in Sehore, Vidisha, and Raisen districts.

faqs.question: Who can apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Any livestock farmer belonging to General, SC, or ST categories residing in eligible districts.

faqs.question: How many buffaloes are provided under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Two Murrah buffaloes are provided to each selected beneficiary.

faqs.question: What is the subsidy amount?
faqs.answer: General category – 50% subsidy; SC/ST category – 75% subsidy.

faqs.question: What is the daily milk yield expected from the buffaloes?
faqs.answer: Each buffalo is expected to produce 8–10 liters of milk per day.

faqs.question: What kind of buffaloes are given?
faqs.answer: Pregnant Murrah buffaloes (at least 5 months) with high milk yield potential.

faqs.question: How are beneficiaries selected?
faqs.answer: Through approvals at Gram Sabha, Janpad Panchayat, and District Panchayat Agricultural Committee levels.

faqs.question: Is there any contribution from the beneficiary?
faqs.answer: Yes, beneficiaries contribute 25% to 50% of the total cost, depending on their category.

faqs.question: What is the role of the district officials?
faqs.answer: District veterinary officers supervise beneficiary selection, animal procurement, and subsidy disbursement.

faqs.question: Where can I apply?
faqs.answer: At the nearest Veterinary Hospital, Animal Dispensary Officer, or Deputy Director of Veterinary Services in your district.
